---
title: "Research"
---

## Publications

- Osamura, K, 2026. [**Bureaucratic appointments and coalition support in presidential systems: An analysis of Brazil's Chamber of Deputies**](https://doi.org/10.1177/13540688261466129). *Party Politics* (forthcoming).\

<br>

## Ongoing

### Work in progress

#### Working papers
  - **Electoral Prospects of Party Defectors: Roll-Call Voting and Electoral Performance in South America.** (*Under review, draft available on request*)
    - Single author.\
<br>
  - **Ideological Cohesion and Legislative Efficiency in Presidential Systems.** (*Draft available on request*)
    - Single author.\
    - Presented at: Japanese Society for Quantitative Political Science 2025 Winter Meeting; 2024 ECPR Summer School on Parliaments; ECPR 2024 General Conference.\


#### Articles
  - **Explaining changes in the distribution of policy competencies in multiparty governments.**
    - With Ulrich Sieberer, Thomas M. Meyer, David Schmuck, Hanna Bãck, Andrea Ceron, Albert Falcó-Gimeno, Benjamin Guinaudeau, Isabelle Guinaudeau, Martin Ejnar Hansen, Kristoffer Kolltveit, Tom Louwerse, Wolfgang C. Müller, and Thomas Persson\
<br>
  - **Tailoring ministerial office to office holders – The impact of gender on portfolio reforms in Western Europe.** (*Title changed for anonimity*)
    - First author. With Thomas M. Meyer, Ulrich Sieberer, and David Schmuck\
    - Presented at the EPSS 2026 Annual Conference.\
<br>
  - **The Politics of Portfolio Design in European Democracies - Research Note on Dataset.** (*Title changed for anonimity*)
    - With David Schmuck, Ulrich Sieberer, and Thomas M. Meyer.\


#### Book chapter
  - **Introduction: Why and How to Study Portfolio Design. In: The Politics of Portfolio Design in European Democracies.** (*Title changed for anonimity*)
    - With Thomas M. Meyer, Ulrich Sieberer, and David Schmuck\

### **Dissertation**
  - **Seeking Office, Policy, or Votes? An Analysis of Legislative Coalitions, motivations, and Behavior in Multiparty Presidential Systems**
    - Supervised by Prof. Thomas M. Meyer, PhD, and Prof. Dr. Laurenz Ennser-Jedenastik.


### **Projects beyond dissertation**
  - **The Politics of Portfolio Design in European Democracies**
    - PI: Thomas Meyer, PhD (University of Vienna) and Dr. Ulrich Sieberer (University of Bamberg).
    - Funding: Austrian Science Fund (grant number: I 5876-G) and German Research Foundation (grant number: SI 1470/9-1)
    - Collaborators: David Schmuck, Hanna Bäck, Andrea Ceron, Albert Falcó-Gimeno, Benjamin Giunaudeau, Isabelle Guinaudeau, Martin Ejnar Hansen, Kristoffer Kolltveit, Tom Louwerse, Wolfgang C. Müller, and Thomas Persson.
    - [https://governmentportfolios.eu/](https://governmentportfolios.eu/)\
    

<br>
<br>

## Conferences, workshops and training

### Conferences

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="70%"}
  - EPSS 2026 Annual Conference (presenter)
:::
::: {.column style="width: 30%; text-align: right;"}
Belfast, United Kingdom\
2026
:::
::::

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="70%"}
- Japanese Society for Quantitative Political Science 2025 Winter Meeting (presenter)
:::
::: {.column style="width: 30%; text-align: right;"}
Tokyo, Japan\
2025
:::
::::


:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="70%"}
- ECPR 2024 General Conference (presenter, 2 papers)
:::
::: {.column style="width: 30%; text-align: right;"}
Dublin, Ireland\
2024
:::
::::


:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="70%"}
- ECPR 8th Conference of the Standing Group on Parliaments (presenter)
:::
::: {.column style="width: 30%; text-align: right;"}
Vienna, Austria\
2023
:::
::::


<!-- :::: {.columns} -->
<!-- ::: {.column width="70%"} -->

<!-- ::: -->
<!-- ::: {.column style="width: 30%; text-align: right;"} -->

<!-- ::: -->
<!-- :::: -->

### Workshops
:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="70%"}
  - Studying Political Actors and Institutions in Brazil: Methodological Approaches and Research Designs
:::
::: {.column style="width: 30%; text-align: right;"}
Online\
2024
:::
::::

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="70%"}
  - University of Vienna Doctoral School of Social Sciences
    - Unleashing the Potential of Geography in Social Sciences: An Introduction to GIS with R

:::
::: {.column style="width: 30%; text-align: right;"}
Vienna, Austria\
2023
:::
::::
<br>

### Summer schools
:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="70%"}
  - Summer School of the ECPR Standing Group on Parliaments (participant, presenter)
:::
::: {.column style="width: 30%; text-align: right;"}
Frankfurt, Germany\
2024
:::
::::

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="70%"}
  - Essex Summer School in Social Science Data Analysis
    - Introduction to Web Scraping and Data Management for Social Scientists
:::
::: {.column style="width: 30%; text-align: right;"}
Colchester, United Kingdom\
2023
:::
::::
<br>

<br>

![[Safely Endangered](https://bsky.app/profile/safelyendangered.bsky.social)](images/comic_hypo.png){fig-align=center width=60%}
